root_text.an10.17:1.5: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sīlavā hoti, pātimokkhasaṁvarasaṁvuto viharati ācāragocarasampanno aṇumattesu vajjesu bhayadassāvī, samādāya sikkhati sikkhāpadesu.
root_text.an10.17:1.6: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sīlavā hoti …pe… samādāya sikkhati sikkhāpadesu,
root_text.an10.17:2.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu bahussuto hoti sutadharo sutasannicayo, ye te dhammā ādikalyāṇā majjhekalyāṇā pariyosānakalyāṇā sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ abhivadanti, tathārūpāssa dhammā bahussutā honti dhātā vacasā paricitā manasānupekkhitā diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā.
root_text.an10.17:2.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu bahussuto hoti …pe… diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā,
root_text.an10.17:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto hoti kalyāṇasahāyo kalyāṇasampavaṅko.
root_text.an10.17:3.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kalyāṇamitto hoti kalyāṇasahāyo kalyāṇasampavaṅko,
root_text.an10.17:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu suvaco hoti sovacassakaraṇehi dhammehi samannāgato, khamo padakkhiṇaggāhī anusāsaniṁ.
root_text.an10.17:4.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu suvaco hoti …pe… anusāsaniṁ,
root_text.an10.17:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yāni tāni sabrahmacārīnaṁ uccāvacāni kiṅkaraṇīyāni, tattha dakkho hoti analaso tatrūpāyāya vīmaṁsāya samannāgato, alaṁ kātuṁ alaṁ saṁvidhātuṁ.
root_text.an10.17:5.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu yāni tāni sabrahmacārīnaṁ …pe… alaṁ kātuṁ alaṁ saṁvidhātuṁ,
root_text.an10.17:6.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhammakāmo hoti piyasamudāhāro, abhidhamme abhivinaye uḷārapāmojjo.
root_text.an10.17:6.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhammakāmo hoti piyasamudāhāro, abhidhamme abhivinaye uḷārapāmojjo,
root_text.an10.17:7.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu āraddhavīriyo viharati akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ pahānāya, kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ upasampadāya, thāmavā daḷhaparakkamo anikkhittadhuro kusalesu dhammesu.
root_text.an10.17:7.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu āraddhavīriyo viharati akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ pahānāya, kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ upasampadāya, thāmavā daḷhaparakkamo anikkhittadhuro kusalesu dhammesu,
root_text.an10.17:8.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu santuṭṭho hoti itarītaracīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānapaccayabhesajjaparikkhārena.
root_text.an10.17:9.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satimā hoti paramena satinepakkena samannāgato cirakatampi cirabhāsitampi saritā anussaritā.
root_text.an10.17:9.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu satimā hoti paramena satinepakkena samannāgato cirakatampi cirabhāsitampi saritā anussaritā,
root_text.an10.17:10.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu paññavā hoti udayatthagāminiyā paññāya samannāgato ariyāya nibbedhikāya sammā dukkhakkhayagāminiyā.
root_text.an10.17:10.2: Yampi, bhikkhave, bhikkhu paññavā hoti udayatthagāminiyā paññāya samannāgato ariyāya nibbedhikāya sammā dukkhakkhayagāminiyā,
root_text.an10.17:11.3: Ime kho, bhikkhave, dasa nāthakaraṇā dhammā”ti.

translation_text.an10.17:1.1: “Mendicants, you should live with a protector, not without one.
translation_text.an10.17:1.2: Living without a protector is suffering.
translation_text.an10.17:1.3: There are ten qualities that serve as protector.
translation_text.an10.17:1.5: Firstly, a mendicant is ethical, restrained in the monastic code, conducting themselves well and resorting for alms in suitable places. Seeing danger in the slightest fault, they keep the rules they’ve undertaken.
translation_text.an10.17:1.7: This is a quality that serves as protector.
translation_text.an10.17:2.1: Furthermore, a mendicant is very learned, remembering and keeping what they’ve learned. These teachings are good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased, describing a spiritual practice that’s entirely full and pure. They are very learned in such teachings, remembering them, rehearsing them, mentally scrutinizing them, and penetrating them theoretically.
translation_text.an10.17:2.3: This too is a quality that serves as protector.
translation_text.an10.17:3.1: Furthermore, a mendicant has good friends, companions, and associates.
translation_text.an10.17:3.3: This too is a quality that serves as protector.
translation_text.an10.17:4.1: Furthermore, a mendicant is easy to admonish, having qualities that make them easy to admonish. They’re patient, and take instruction respectfully.
translation_text.an10.17:4.3: This too is a quality that serves as protector.
translation_text.an10.17:5.1: Furthermore, a mendicant is deft and tireless in a diverse spectrum of duties for their spiritual companions, understanding how to go about things in order to complete and organize the work.
translation_text.an10.17:5.3: This too is a quality that serves as protector.
translation_text.an10.17:6.1: Furthermore, a mendicant loves the teachings and is a delight to converse with, being full of joy in the teaching and training.
translation_text.an10.17:6.3: This too is a quality that serves as protector.
translation_text.an10.17:7.1: Furthermore, a mendicant lives with energy roused up for giving up unskillful qualities and embracing skillful qualities. They are strong, staunchly vigorous, not slacking off when it comes to developing skillful qualities.
translation_text.an10.17:7.3: This too is a quality that serves as protector.
translation_text.an10.17:8.1: Furthermore, a mendicant is content with any kind of robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick.
translation_text.an10.17:8.3: This too is a quality that serves as protector.
translation_text.an10.17:9.1: Furthermore, a mendicant is mindful. They have utmost mindfulness and alertness, and can remember and recall what was said and done long ago.
translation_text.an10.17:9.3: This too is a quality that serves as protector.
translation_text.an10.17:10.1: Furthermore, a mendicant is wise. They have the wisdom of arising and passing away which is noble, penetrative, and leads to the complete ending of suffering.
translation_text.an10.17:10.3: This too is a quality that serves as protector.
translation_text.an10.17:11.1: You should live with a protector, not without one.
translation_text.an10.17:11.2: Living without a protector is suffering.
translation_text.an10.17:11.3: These are the ten qualities that serve as protector.”

variant_text.an10.17:2.1: sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ → sātthā sabyañjanā (bj) | bahussutā → bahū sutā (?)
